entries.evidence_summary: Authority uses a strict precedence chain with three operative layers and a separate 'Reference evidence' heading for static docs. No governance documents appear as reporting-chain superiors.

'Material pushback' is defined once in <guardrails> as 'any unresolved reviewer conceptual group with blocking or major severity.' All downstream references (Step 5, completion) use this definition without rephrasing.

<outputs> uses two distinct labeled tables: 'Canonical artifacts promoted on frozen or frozen_with_conditions' and 'Run-scoped evidence rooted at runs/<job-id>/' with full tool-relative paths in every row.

Step 5 now defines five mutually exclusive predicates evaluated in priority order with explicit observable conditions. 'blocked' is scoped to mechanical/structural failures only; 'pushback' requires structural validation to have succeeded first, eliminating overlap.
Step 3 specifies semantic concept matching (not literal string equality) for out-of-scope verification, and requires the self-check to record evidence for each executed check.
The Out of scope list contains six distinct, non-overlapping exclusions with no duplicate entries or contradictory phrasings.

Arbitration is defined with: trigger condition (after inherited split-verdict path exhausted), actor (the leader), evidence requirements (arbitration_used, arbitration_rationale, deferred item IDs in result.json), and limitation (minor/suggestion only; may never resolve blocking or major items).
Every canonical artifact row uses a full tool-relative path (e.g., tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-governance-smoke-role.md). No basename-only references remain.
<completion> defines testable completion criteria for all five terminal states (frozen, frozen_with_conditions, pushback, resume_required, blocked), each with the specific artifacts that must exist.

<context-gathering> is labeled 'Preconditions before Step 1' with its own numbered list. Step 1 begins the execution sequence separately, eliminating ambiguity about whether context-gathering is step 0 or step 1.

All cross-round tracking now uses 'conceptual-group ID' as the canonical identity. Step 4 item 2 keys fix-items-map.json by reviewer conceptual-group ID. Step 4 item 6 carries unresolved conceptual groups forward by conceptual-group ID. Guardrails and outputs tables use the same terminology.

<guardrails> explicitly states 'Preserve decision history across update and fix operations by appending a dated section to the canonical decision log. Prior decision-log entries are never deleted.' Step 5 promotion rules repeat 'append a dated section.'
Every step header carries the label '(target-state required behavior)', clearly marking all steps as describing the intended target state rather than current implementation.

Step 4 item 5 explicitly states learning.json is written 'even on the terminal clean round.' Guardrails declares learning.json, compliance-map.json, and diff-summary.json as universal per-round artifacts with no revision-specific guard.

<outputs> provides one authoritative artifact matrix with two tables. Every artifact has its artifact class, full canonical tool-relative path or root, and write lifecycle. No artifact is declared in multiple places with conflicting paths.
<authority> names the exact governing sources in precedence order and uses 'Inherited runtime obligations from the active contract' to reference shared policy rather than restate it locally.
<role> describes the standing mission ('create, update, and repair governed ADF agent role packages') without reference to current run state, resume conditions, or round-specific context.

Writable surface is anchored to the active runtime contract and lists exact tool-relative roots: tools/agent-role-builder/role/ and tools/agent-role-builder/runs/<job-id>/. Static documents are explicitly excluded from granting operative write authority.

Operative authority lists only binding governance sources (COO controller, runtime contract, component governance files). Reference documents are in a separate 'Reference evidence' section. No request-time settings appear in the durable authority model.

Review-mode vocabulary (full, delta, regression_sanity) is sourced from the active contract's review_modes field. Step 4 item 1 now uses 'regression_sanity sweep' for the final clean round, matching the contract vocabulary exactly. The duplicate 'full sweep again' phrasing has been removed.
The Out of scope section contains only exclusion statements. No positive clarifications or inclusion semantics appear in the exclusions list.

Step 4 defines an ordered seven-item round sub-sequence: compliance mapping, fix-items mapping (when applicable), reviewer verdict production, split-verdict handling, learning engine, rulebook walk with diff, and post-round postmortem refresh. Each sub-step names the artifact it writes.

<authority> lists the three component-local governance files (rulebook.json, review-prompt.json, review-contract.json) and uses 'Inherited runtime obligations from the active contract' to avoid restating shared policy. <context-gathering> precondition 2 loads all three files at start.

<inputs> enumerates all seven required prerequisites: request JSON, source_refs, runtime review contract (including shared contract extension), component governance files, active reviewer roster, governance configuration (review budget, split-verdict, freeze rules, pushback), and runtime configuration (execution mode, watchdog timeout, launch attempts, job ID, active review mode).
